root_text.sn47.38:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāye kāyānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.38:1.4: Tassa kāye kāyānupassino viharato kāyo pariññāto hoti.
root_text.sn47.38:2.1: Vedanāsu vedanānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.38:2.2: Tassa vedanāsu vedanānupassino viharato vedanā pariññātā honti.
root_text.sn47.38:3.1: Citte cittānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.38:3.2: Tassa citte cittānupassino viharato cittaṁ pariññātaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn47.38:4.1: Dhammesu dhammānupassī viharati ātāpī sampajāno satimā, vineyya loke abhijjhādomanassaṁ.
root_text.sn47.38:4.2: Tassa dhammesu dhammānupassino viharato dhammā pariññātā honti.

translation_text.sn47.38:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn47.38:1.3: It’s when a mendicant meditates by observing an aspect of the body—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.38:1.4: As they do so they completely understand the body.
translation_text.sn47.38:1.5: When the body is completely understood they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.38:2.1: They meditate observing an aspect of feelings—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.38:2.2: As they do so they completely understand feelings.
translation_text.sn47.38:2.3: When feelings are completely understood they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.38:3.1: They meditate observing an aspect of the mind—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.38:3.2: As they do so they completely understand the mind.
translation_text.sn47.38:3.3: When the mind is completely understood they realize freedom from death.
translation_text.sn47.38:4.1: They meditate observing an aspect of principles—keen, aware, and mindful, rid of covetousness and displeasure for the world.
translation_text.sn47.38:4.2: As they do so they completely understand principles.
translation_text.sn47.38:4.3: When principles are completely understood they realize freedom from death.”
